questions.text: The SI unit of work is:
questions.explanation: Work is measured in Joules (J). 1 Joule = 1 Newton-meter (N·m)
Kinetic energy KE = (1/2)mv², where m is mass and v is velocity.

questions.text: Power is the rate of doing ________.
questions.explanation: Power is defined as the rate of doing work or rate of transfer of energy. P = W/t

questions.correctAnswer: The work-energy theorem states that the change in kinetic energy of an object equals the net work done on it.
questions.explanation: Work-energy theorem: W_net = ΔKE = KE_final - KE_initial
